digest.component_or_area: compaction fold rollback path and fsck reconciliation
propositions: The compaction rollback path only logs a failed reverse-rename then unconditionally removes the snapshot, causing silent undetectable data loss that violates the all-or-none invariant I1.
The fix retains the snapshot on any reverse-rename failure during rollback, skipping os.remove and emitting a 'rollback incomplete; run fsck' diagnostic before returning failure.
With the snapshot retained, the residual mixed state is already detectable and repairable by existing fsck via SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT and --repair-snapshots, requiring no fsck code change.
Reads in the mixed window are already correct because the reversed-to-active source sorts before the retained snapshot and the positional skip prevents double-counting the folded event.
fsck --repair-snapshots reconciles by rebuilding a new snapshot from the full ordered log and retiring the live raw source, and must not flag a healthy fully-compacted ticket.
Fault-injection tests must be added for the forward-rename and reverse-rename loops, which the negative suite currently omits.
